title: IsolationForest::score_samples returns raw anomaly score (higher=outlier); sklearn convention is negated (higher=inlier)
description:
ferrolearn FittedIsolationForest::score_samples returns 2^(-mean_path/c_n), the raw anomaly score where higher = more anomalous. sklearn IsolationForest.score_samples returns the NEGATED form so higher = more inlier (matches LocalOutlierFactor convention).

Fix: negate the returned scores. Also expose a separate decision_function (= score_samples - offset) for full sklearn parity.

Predict() is unaffected — it already returns -1 for outliers, +1 for inliers correctly. Only the score_samples sign convention is inverted.
